chunk_summary: This section focuses on selecting induction therapy for eosinophilic esophagitis (EoE) and defining endpoints, emphasizing therapeutic elimination diets and pediatric management guidelines based on recent evidence.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of therapeutic elimination diets in EoE induction therapy.
Identify key endpoints for evaluating the success of EoE induction therapy.
Review pediatric-specific guidelines for EoE diagnosis and management.
Explore evidence-based approaches to food elimination in EoE treatment.
key_facts: EoE affects approximately 1 in 2,000 children, with increasing prevalence globally.
Therapeutic elimination diets are a cornerstone of EoE management, often targeting 4-6 common allergens (e.g., milk, wheat, soy, eggs, nuts, seafood).
Endoscopic and histologic improvement are primary endpoints for assessing therapy success, with <15 eosinophils per high-power field considered remission.
Pediatric guidelines recommend a multidisciplinary approach, including dietitians and allergists, for effective EoE management.
Recent studies highlight the importance of tailoring elimination diets to individual allergen profiles for optimal outcomes.
reference_quote: Therapeutic elimination diets targeting common allergens, such as milk and wheat, are effective in achieving histologic remission in pediatric eosinophilic esophagitis. (Source: ESPGHAN, 2024)
